sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Info action to introduce ideas for reforming Cardano treasury governance into public discussion and encourage collaboration on improving the treasury governance process.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Sketch basic ideas for treasury governance reform, Outline guardrails for strategic direction, Propose a treasury governance process involving NCL, budget, and overall treasury withdrawal votes, Propose a thematic budget structure, Encourage ecosystem collaboration on refinements, amendments, and possible constitutional changes

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support in the existing governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, ill will, and strife within the community.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says treasury governance is pivotal for Cardano's growth and stability and should provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the governance system and the constitution.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says the NCL should not be treated as a potential overall budget and is only a vague initial approximation for a budget.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says DReps should vote on dedicated and balanced budgets rather than isolated individual project funding decisions.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support in the existing governance system.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, ill will, and strife within the community.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says treasury governance is pivotal for Cardano's growth and stability and should provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says the NCL should not be treated as a potential overall budget and is only a vague initial approximation for a budget.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal says treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the governance system and the constitution." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support in the existing governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
